data.title: TECHNOLOGY | Uncertainty Surrounds Bank of America's Tech Infrastructure Investment
data.description: Bank of America International President Bernard Mensah has expressed uncertainty regarding the returns from the bank's significant investments in technology infrastructure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Mensah's remarks about the unpredictable nature of the payoff from these expenditures. Despite the substantial financial commitment, the outcomes of such investments remain unclear, reflecting broader industry challenges in forecasting the benefits of technological advancements. Mensah's comments underscore the complexities faced by financial institutions as they navigate the evolving landscape of tech infrastructure development.

data.title: California Introduces Digital Financial Assets Law for Crypto Regulation
data.description:
California's Department of Financial Protection and Innovation (DFPI) has released an update on the implementation of the Digital Financial Assets Law (DFAL), requiring all individuals or companies providing crypto-related services to California residents to obtain a DFAL license, submit a license application, or meet exemption conditions by July 1, 2026, or face enforcement actions. According to BlockBeats, the DFAL, signed into law by California Governor Gavin Newsom in October 2023, establishes a statewide licensing and regulatory framework for digital assets, including various digital asset services and crypto ATMs. This system is widely compared to New York's BitLicense introduced in 2015.

The DFAL license applications will open on March 9, 2026, through the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), and regulatory authorities recommend that businesses review the checklist and participate in industry training on March 23.

California accounts for about a quarter of all blockchain enterprises in the United States. Joe Ciccolo, Executive Director of the California Blockchain Advocacy Coalition (CBAC), noted that as the world's fourth-largest economy, California's regulatory approach could drive a unified compliance standard across the U.S. "Clear and predictable rules help attract serious operators and institutional capital," he stated, but also warned that overly aggressive enforcement or regulations disconnected from industry realities might lead some businesses to exit the California market or move overseas.

data.title: Ark Invest buys $6.9 million worth of Coinbase stock.
data.description: On Tuesday, Ark Invest purchased 41,453 Coinbase shares, worth approximately $69,000, through three of its exchange-traded funds (ETFs). Specifically, the purchases were as follows: 29,689 shares in the Innovation ETF (ARKK), worth $4.9 million; 7,525 shares in the Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), worth $1.2 million; and 4,239 shares in the ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF), worth $704,000. This move reverses the firm's earlier-month reduction in Coinbase holdings. Currently, Coinbase shares account for 4%, 3.7%, and 5.6% of the holdings in the ARKK, ARKW, and ARKF funds, respectively.

data.title: ECONOMY | U.S. GDP Growth Anticipated Amid Employment Concerns
data.description: Forecasters predict that the upcoming report on Friday will reveal a robust expansion of the U.S. GDP in 2025. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over stagnant employment levels, which are drawing comparisons to the 'jobless recovery' experienced in the early 2000s. Economists are closely monitoring these trends, as they could impact future economic stability and growth. The anticipated GDP growth reflects positive economic activity, but the lack of corresponding employment growth raises questions about the sustainability of this expansion. Analysts suggest that while the economy shows signs of strength, the labor market's sluggishness could pose challenges moving forward.

data.title: UK Inflation Rate Falls to 3% in January, Meeting Expectations
data.description:
The United Kingdom's inflation rate decreased to 3% in January, aligning with market expectations and marking a 10-month low. According to RTHK, this decline represents a 0.4 percentage point drop from the previous month, driven by slower price increases in transportation, food, and non-alcoholic beverages. The easing inflation has strengthened market anticipation that the Bank of England may soon resume interest rate cuts.

Last month's Consumer Price Index (CPI) fell by 0.5% month-on-month, as expected, following a 0.4% increase in the previous month. Excluding energy, food, and tobacco, the core CPI rose by 3.1% year-on-year, slightly above expectations but still the lowest since September 2021. On a monthly basis, the core CPI decreased by 0.6%, slightly less than the market's forecasted 0.7% drop.

data.title: Russia Seeks to Prevent Fuel Shortages After Druzhba Supply Halt
data.description: Russia is taking measures to avert fuel shortages following the cessation of supplies through the Druzhba pipeline on January 27. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's efforts to stabilize the fuel supply chain. The halt in the Druzhba pipeline, a critical conduit for oil, has prompted Russian authorities to implement strategies to ensure adequate fuel availability. The government is focusing on alternative supply routes and increasing domestic production to mitigate potential disruptions. These actions are part of a broader initiative to maintain energy stability and prevent any adverse impacts on the economy. The situation is being closely monitored to ensure that fuel demands are met without significant interruptions.

data.title: AI's Impact on Game Development Highlighted by Supercell CEO
data.description: Supercell CEO Ilkka Paananen expressed strong confidence in the potential of artificial intelligence to enhance human creativity. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Paananen's belief that AI will elevate creative capabilities to unprecedented heights. He emphasized the transformative 'superpowers' AI provides to game developers, suggesting a significant impact on the industry.

data.title: Cuba Reaffirms Commitment to Combat Drug Trafficking Amid International Tensions
data.description: On February 17, local time, the Cuban Ministry of Justice held a press conference in Havana to discuss the progress of its efforts to prevent and combat drug-related crimes by 2025. According to Jin10, during the conference, the Cuban Ministry of Justice reiterated that Cuba is not a drug-producing country and dismissed claims that it poses a threat to U.S. national security. Rosa Bel Calderón Verde, Cuba's Minister of Justice and Chair of the National Drug Commission, emphasized Cuba's unwavering commitment to fighting drug proliferation and illegal trafficking despite complex international relations. Officials from Cuba's anti-drug and border forces highlighted ongoing international cooperation, noting that Cuba continues to share relevant intelligence with the United States, effectively serving as a crucial line of defense against drug trafficking.

data.title: Bitwise CIO says DeFi could lead market out of crypto winter as Strategy’s Saylor predicts bitcoin recovery
data.description:
Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan said DeFi could help lead the market out of crypto winter, while Michael Saylor declared "spring is coming. "
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/390292/bitwise-cio-says-defi-could-lead-market-out-of-crypto-winter-as-strategys-saylor-predicts-bitcoin-recovery?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Demand for Additional Tier 1 Bonds Reaches New Heights
data.description: A rare issuer has set a new record in a crucial value metric for Additional Tier 1 bonds this week, highlighting the strong demand for this high-risk category of bank debt. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the significance of this development in the financial markets. Additional Tier 1 bonds, known for their riskiness, have seen increased interest from investors seeking higher returns. This surge in demand reflects broader trends in the banking sector, where investors are willing to take on more risk for potentially greater rewards. The record-setting issuance underscores the evolving dynamics in the bond market, as financial institutions navigate changing economic conditions and investor appetites.

data.title: Brevan Howard's crypto fund suffered a 30% loss in 2025, marking its worst performance since inception.
data.description: Brevan Howard's crypto fund, BH Digital Asset, saw its value shrink by 29.5% in 2025, marking its worst annual performance since its inception in 2021. The fund primarily invests in companies related to crypto tokens and digital assets, and previously achieved growth of 43% and 52% in 2023 and 2024, respectively. During the same period, Bitcoin fell by 6%, meaning the fund underperformed Bitcoin.

data.title: BlackRock's iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF Plans Significant ETH Staking
data.description:
BlackRock intends to stake between 70% and 95% of Ethereum (ETH) in its iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHB) to generate yield for investors. According to NS3.AI, the ETF will impose an 18% fee on gross staking rewards, in addition to a 0.25% sponsor fee. This strategy could potentially yield $11 to $20 million annually if scaled effectively.

Ethereum's staking protocol presents certain constraints, as exits and reward activation can take several weeks. These factors may impact liquidity and the pricing dynamics of ETF shares.

data.title: Why Is Nvidia Moving Higher in Premarket? New Multi-Year Meta AI Chip Deal
data.description:
Nvidia shares rose in premarket trading after the company announced a multiyear deal to supply Meta with millions of AI chips, expanding its role in powering Meta’s data centers.
source: https://tokenist.com/why-is-nvidia-moving-higher-in-premarket-new-multi-year-meta-ai-chip-deal/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=why-is-nvidia-moving-higher-in-premarket-new-multi-year-meta-ai-chip-deal

data.title: Bitcoin's Historical Interaction with Production Cost
data.description: Crypto KOL Satoshi Flipper posted on X about Bitcoin's historical price movements in relation to its production cost. According to the post, Bitcoin has touched its production cost average nine times over the past 14 years. On four occasions, the price dipped below this average but did not remain there for long.

data.title: USMCA Pact's Importance to U.S. and Mexican Economies
data.description: The economic integration between the United States and Mexico has reached a level where dismantling the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) would have significant repercussions for Washington. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the potential impact of unraveling the trade agreement is underestimated by U.S. President Donald Trump. The USMCA, which replaced the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), plays a crucial role in maintaining economic stability and fostering trade relations between the two nations. The agreement's dissolution could lead to economic disruptions, affecting various sectors reliant on cross-border trade. Experts emphasize the importance of the USMCA in sustaining the economic ties that have developed over the years, warning against underestimating the potential fallout of its termination.

data.title: Bitcoin's Historical Pattern Suggests Potential Bullish Phase Ahead
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X, highlighting a historical pattern in Bitcoin's performance. The cryptocurrency has experienced five consecutive months of decline, a situation reminiscent of a previous period that led to a significant bullish phase. Observers are speculating whether this pattern might repeat, potentially signaling an upcoming surge in Bitcoin's value. The discussion centers around the possibility of a rebound, drawing parallels to past market behavior. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, considering various factors that could influence Bitcoin's trajectory in the coming months.

data.title: Verizon Plans Sale of Euro and Sterling Hybrid Bonds
data.description: Verizon Communications Inc. is preparing to issue euro and sterling denominated hybrid bonds on Wednesday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the telecommunications giant's strategic move to tap into the European debt market. This decision comes as part of Verizon's broader financial strategy to diversify its funding sources and optimize its capital structure. The hybrid bonds, which combine elements of both debt and equity, are expected to attract significant interest from investors seeking stable returns amid fluctuating market conditions. Verizon's initiative reflects a growing trend among U.S. companies to explore international bond markets, leveraging favorable interest rates and investor appetite for diversified financial instruments. The issuance is anticipated to bolster Verizon's financial flexibility and support its ongoing operational and strategic objectives.

data.title: Hong Kong-based Laure made a single purchase of $436 million in IBIT, while the US spot Bitcoin ETF saw an outflow of $104 million on Tuesday.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, the US spot Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of $104 million, reducing its total trading volume to approximately $3 billion. The SEC's Q4 holdings report showed that Hong Kong-based Laurore made a single purchase of $436 million worth of iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT); Jane Street bought $276 million worth of IBIT in Q4, becoming its second-largest buyer. Additionally, Mubadala Investment increased its IBIT holdings to 12.7 million shares, worth $630 million. During the same period, Brevan Howard reduced its IBIT holdings by approximately 85%, down to $273 million; Goldman Sachs reduced its IBIT holdings by approximately 40%, leaving a remaining holding of approximately $1 billion.

data.title: Palantir’s Peter Thiel Dumps ETHZilla as Company Abandons Ethereum Treasury Model
data.description:
Key Takeaways Peter Thiel has completely exited ETHZilla, reducing his stake from 7. 5% to 0%.
source: https://coindoo.com/palantirs-peter-thiel-dumps-ethzilla-as-company-abandons-ethereum-treasury-model/

data.title: Zimbabwe's Tobacco Production Forecast to Exceed 360 Million Kilograms This Year
data.description: Zimbabwe anticipates a significant increase in tobacco production, projecting over 360 million kilograms for this year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's optimistic outlook for its tobacco industry. This forecast marks a substantial rise from previous years, reflecting improvements in agricultural practices and favorable weather conditions. Tobacco remains a crucial export commodity for Zimbabwe, contributing significantly to the nation's economy. The government and industry stakeholders are hopeful that this year's production will bolster economic growth and enhance the livelihoods of farmers across the country.
